title: Symbol as Points: Panoptic Symbol Spotting via Point-based Representation
abstract: This work studies the problem of panoptic symbol spotting, which is to spot and parse both countable object instances (windows, doors, tables, etc.) and uncountable stuff (wall, railing, etc.) from CAD drawings. Rather than rasterizing or using graph methods, the approach treats graphic primitives as a set of 2D points and applies point cloud segmentation techniques with a point transformer and mask2former-like head.
method_summary: SymPoint converts CAD vector graphics into point representations, then leverages point cloud segmentation with attention-with-connection module, contrastive connection learning, and KNN interpolation for mask attention, achieving 9.6% PQ improvement over prior work on FloorPlanCAD.

title: SymPoint Revolutionized: Boosting Panoptic Symbol Spotting with Layer Feature Enhancement
abstract: SymPoint-V2 improves upon SymPoint by incorporating a Layer Feature-Enhanced module that encodes graphical layer data into feature representations, and a Position-Guided Training method that accelerates model convergence. Demonstrates superior performance and faster training on public benchmarks.
method_summary: Two innovations: (1) LFE module encoding layer information into primitive features, and (2) position-guided training improving early-stage learning dynamics, addressing SymPoint's limitations in layer information utilization.

title: CADSpotting: Robust Panoptic Symbol Spotting on Large-Scale CAD Drawings
abstract: Introduces CADSpotting for panoptic symbol spotting in large-scale architectural CAD drawings. Handles symbol diversity, scale variations, and overlapping elements through point cloud representation. Novel Sliding Window Aggregation technique enables accurate segmentation in large drawings. Releases LS-CAD dataset of 45 annotated floorplans.
method_summary: Represents primitives as densely sampled points with coordinate attributes, uses unified 3D point cloud model for feature learning, and incorporates Sliding Window Aggregation combining weighted voting and NMS for large-scale drawing segmentation.

title: ArchCAD-400K: A Large-Scale CAD Drawings Dataset and New Baseline for Panoptic Symbol Spotting
abstract: Introduces automated CAD annotation engine and ArchCAD-400K dataset containing 413,062 chunks from 5,538 standardized drawings, over 26x larger than existing datasets. Proposes Dual-Pathway Symbol Spotter (DPSS) with adaptive fusion for enhanced panoptic symbol spotting.
method_summary: Automated annotation engine leveraging intrinsic CAD drawing attributes combined with DPSS model using adaptive fusion to integrate primitive features with complementary image features.

title: Point or Line? Using Line-based Representation for Panoptic Symbol Spotting in CAD Drawings
abstract: Proposes VecFormer using line-based representation of primitives to preserve geometric continuity. Branch Fusion Refinement module integrates instance and semantic predictions, achieving 91.1 PQ with significant improvements in Stuff-PQ metrics.
method_summary: VecFormer replaces point-based approaches with line-based representation preserving structural information while reducing computational cost. Includes fusion module reconciling instance and semantic segmentation outputs.

title: FloorPlanCAD: A Large-Scale CAD Drawing Dataset for Panoptic Symbol Spotting
abstract: Introduces FloorPlanCAD dataset of over 10,000 floor plans with vector-based annotations across 30 object categories. Defines panoptic symbol spotting task and proposes CNN-GCN hybrid method combining graph and convolutional networks.
method_summary: Merges Graph Convolutional Networks with CNNs to capture both non-Euclidean and Euclidean features in an end-to-end trainable architecture for thing instance and stuff semantic detection.

title: GAT-CADNet: Graph Attention Network for Panoptic Symbol Spotting in CAD Drawings
abstract: Models CAD drawings as graphs and uses graph attention networks with relative spatial encoding for vertex relationships and cascaded edge encoding for adjacency matrix prediction, enabling unified panoptic symbol spotting.
method_summary: Graph-based approach treating CAD drawings as geometric primitives. Uses GAT with relative spatial encoding and cascaded edge encoding to predict adjacency matrices for instance detection.

title: Text-Enhanced Panoptic Symbol Spotting in CAD Drawings
abstract: Incorporates textual annotations alongside geometric primitives for symbol spotting. Constructs unified representations by jointly modeling geometric and textual primitives using a Transformer backbone with type-aware attention mechanisms.
method_summary: Combines CNN-extracted visual features with Transformer-based architecture enhanced by type-aware attention, explicitly modeling relationships between different primitive types for comprehensive drawing interpretation.

title: VectorGraphNET: Graph Attention Networks for Accurate Segmentation of Complex Technical Drawings
abstract: Extracts vector data from technical drawings in PDF format by converting to SVG and constructing feature-rich graph representation. Applies graph attention transformers with hierarchical labels for line-level segmentation, achieving SOTA on FloorPlanCAD.
method_summary: Converts PDFs to SVG, creates graph capturing relationships between vector entities using geometric information, then applies graph attention transformer with hierarchical label definitions for semantic segmentation at line level.

title: Automatic Detection and Classification of Symbols in Engineering Drawings
abstract: Proposes a method for finding and classifying components in design diagrams using multiple deep neural networks. Pre-trained on design templates to learn transferable features, enabling work on unseen templates without additional training.
method_summary: Uses deep neural networks to locate objects from legend tables and extract position, count, and metadata. Pre-training on design templates enables transfer to unseen layouts for design validation and component counting.

title: Fine-Tuning Vision-Language Model for Automated Engineering Drawing Information Extraction
abstract: Fine-tunes Florence-2 (0.23B parameters) on 400 annotated engineering drawings for GD&T extraction. Compared against GPT-4o and Claude-3.5-Sonnet zero-shot, achieves 29.95% precision increase, 37.75% recall increase, 52.40% F1 improvement, and 43.15% hallucination reduction.
method_summary: Full-parameter fine-tuning of Florence-2 VLM across three experiments with augmented datasets, evaluated against closed-source baselines in zero-shot settings for GD&T information extraction.

title: A Multi-Stage Hybrid Framework for Automated Interpretation of Multi-View Engineering Drawings Using Vision Language Model
abstract: Three-stage pipeline: YOLOv11-det for layout segmentation, YOLOv11-obb for orientation-aware annotation detection (measures, GD&T, surface roughness), and two Donut-based VLMs for semantic parsing. Achieves F1 scores of 0.672 and 0.963 for textual and quantitative content respectively.
method_summary: Combines object detection (YOLOv11 variants) for layout and annotation localization with vision language models for semantic content extraction, producing structured JSON output.

title: Automated Parsing of Engineering Drawings for Structured Information Extraction Using a Fine-tuned Document Understanding Transformer
abstract: Combines YOLOv11 for detecting nine categories of drawing elements with fine-tuned Donut transformer for structured JSON output. Single unified model achieves 94.77% precision for GD&T with 97.3% F1 score.
method_summary: Integrates YOLOv11 detection with fine-tuned Donut document understanding transformer on cropped detections, comparing single vs category-specific models.

title: CadVLM: Bridging Language and Vision in the Generation of Parametric CAD Sketches
abstract: End-to-end vision language model for CAD generation adapting pre-trained foundation models for engineering sketch manipulation. First multimodal LLM successfully applied to parametric CAD generation, supporting autocompletion, autoconstraint, and conditional generation.
method_summary: Leverages pre-trained NLP and CV foundation models, combining sketch primitive sequences with sketch images as multimodal inputs for generative CAD modeling.

title: AECV-Bench: Benchmarking Multimodal Models on Architectural and Engineering Drawings Understanding
abstract: Benchmark for evaluating VLMs on architectural and engineering drawings. Two tasks: counting objects on 120 floor plans and answering drawing-grounded questions (192 QA pairs). Models excel at text extraction (0.95 accuracy) but struggle with symbol recognition and spatial reasoning (0.40-0.55 accuracy).
method_summary: Object counting with exact-match accuracy and MAPE metrics, plus document QA with LLM-as-a-judge scoring and human adjudication, revealing capability gradient from text-centric to drawing literacy tasks.

title: AEC-Bench: A Multimodal Benchmark for Agentic Systems in Architecture, Engineering, and Construction
abstract: Multimodal benchmark evaluating agentic systems on real-world AEC tasks including drawing understanding, cross-sheet reasoning, and project-level coordination. 196 task instances across 9 task families. Released under Apache 2 license.
method_summary: Systematic benchmarking framework testing multiple foundation model harnesses (Claude Code, Codex) to identify consistent tools and architectural patterns improving AEC domain performance.

title: Blueprint-Bench: Comparing Spatial Intelligence of LLMs, Agents and Image Models
abstract: Benchmark evaluating spatial reasoning by converting apartment photographs into 2D floor plans. Tests GPT-5, Claude 4 Opus, Gemini 2.5 Pro, Grok-4 on 50 apartments. Most models perform at or below random baseline; agent-based approaches show no meaningful improvement.
method_summary: Custom scoring algorithm comparing generated floor plans to ground truth using room connectivity graphs and size rankings across language models, image generation models, and agent systems.

key_results.finding: most models at or below random baseline

title: DRC-Coder: Automated DRC Checker Code Generation Using LLM Autonomous Agent
abstract: Multi-agent framework combining VLMs and LLMs to interpret foundry specifications and generate DRC implementations. Auto-evaluation function for debugging. Achieves perfect F1 score (1.000) on sub-3nm standard cell layouts vs standard prompting (F1=0.631), with 4-minute average generation time per rule.
method_summary: Two specialized LLMs with vision capabilities process textual, visual, and layout data for rule interpretation and code generation, with automated evaluation for iterative bug fixing.

title: ARCEAK: An Automated Rule Checking Framework Enhanced with Architectural Knowledge
abstract: Automated rule checking in construction using GPT to streamline interpretation of regulatory texts and generate model compliance checking code. Two-stage processing: rule extraction from regulatory text and automatic verification code generation.
method_summary: Two-stage AI pipeline: (1) extracting rule information from regulatory text using language models, (2) generating verification code automatically, replacing manual rule interpretation.

title: Automated Facility Enumeration for Building Compliance Checking using Door Detection and Large Language Models
abstract: Combines door detection with LLM-based Chain-of-Thought reasoning for automated facility enumeration in building compliance checking. Demonstrates effectiveness across diverse datasets and both real-world and synthetic floor plan data.
method_summary: Computer vision door detection combined with LLM Chain-of-Thought prompting to automatically enumerate facility types and validate quantities against regulatory requirements.

title: CODE-ACCORD: A Corpus of Building Regulatory Data for Rule Generation towards Automatic Compliance Checking
abstract: Dataset of 862 annotated sentences from building regulations in England and Finland, with 4,297 entities and 4,329 relations. Supports NLP tasks including text classification, entity recognition, and relation extraction for automated compliance checking in AEC.
method_summary: Manual annotation of regulatory sentences by 12 annotators creating ground-truth dataset applicable to deep neural networks and LLMs for compliance automation.

title: Context-Aware Mapping of 2D Drawing Annotations to 3D CAD Features Using LLM-Assisted Reasoning for Manufacturing Automation
abstract: Framework mapping 2D drawing entities to 3D CAD features for manufacturing automation. Combines deterministic scoring with LLM reasoning for ambiguities. Achieves 83.67% precision and 90.46% recall on 20 real CAD-drawing pairs.
method_summary: Deterministic-first pipeline with semantic enrichment, type compatibility scoring, and LLM escalation for unresolved cases, prioritizing interpretability for industrial manufacturing.

title: AECBench: A Hierarchical Benchmark for Knowledge Evaluation of Large Language Models in the AEC Field
abstract: Comprehensive benchmark assessing LLM capabilities in AEC domain. Five-level cognition-oriented evaluation framework (Knowledge Memorization through Application), 23 evaluation tasks, 4,800-question dataset. Reveals performance declines across cognitive levels with weaknesses in table interpretation and complex reasoning.
method_summary: Hierarchical evaluation framework with five cognitive levels, authentic AEC tasks, and LLM-as-a-Judge methodology with expert-derived rubrics for assessing nine LLMs.

title: Enginuity: Building an Open Multi-Domain Dataset of Complex Engineering Diagrams
abstract: Proposes Enginuity, the first open large-scale multi-domain engineering diagram dataset with comprehensive structural annotations for automated diagram parsing. Covers P&IDs, electrical schematics, and architectural drawings with >10K real-world diagrams containing both component and structural relationship annotations.
method_summary: Multi-domain annotation framework covering symbols, connections, and structural relationships across engineering diagram types. Provides standardized evaluation protocols for diagram understanding tasks.

title: Automatic Building Code Review
abstract: Framework for automated extraction of geometric attributes (surface area, tilt, insulation values), parsing of operational schedules, and compliance verification against building energy codes.
method_summary: Automated pipeline extracting geometric and operational attributes from building models and verifying compliance against regulatory codes using rule-based and ML-assisted checking.
